findings.title: Wide data tables (rates matrix, expenses/settlements) require easy-to-miss horizontal scroll on phones
findings.symptom: On a narrow phone the currency×year rates matrix (and long expense/settlement rows) overflow the viewport; the only way to see right-hand columns is a horizontal swipe inside the wrapper, with no visible affordance that more content exists off-screen.
findings.rootCause: The matrix is laid out at width:max-content inside an overflow-x scroll wrapper, so it scrolls rather than reflowing, but there is no edge-fade / scrollbar / 'scroll for more' cue, and a horizontal drag inside it competes with the page.
findings.repro: On a ≤400px screen open Settings → rates editor with 3+ currencies and 3+ years; right-hand year columns are off-screen with no indication.
findings.suggestedFix: Add a right-edge gradient fade + a thin persistent scrollbar on the wrap, or a card/stacked layout under ~480px.

findings.title: Keyboard-avoidance only lifts modal bottom-sheets, not focused inline inputs in long non-modal forms
findings.symptom: On iOS, focusing an input near the bottom of a long NON-modal page (e.g. an inline form below the fold) can leave it under the on-screen keyboard; --kb-offset is only consumed by .modal-overlay.
findings.rootCause: _update() writes --kb-offset globally but only the .modal-overlay rule uses it (padding-bottom); non-modal inputs rely on the browser's native scroll-into-view, which iOS does imperfectly for custom-scrolled documents.
findings.repro: iOS Safari: open a long page with a text input near the bottom (non-modal), tap it — the field can sit behind the keyboard.
findings.suggestedFix: On focusin of an input/textarea, scrollIntoView({block:'center'}) when --kb-offset>0; or apply the offset to the scroll container, not just modals.

findings.title: Pull-to-refresh trigger threshold (~140px of finger travel) is heavier than native
findings.symptom: The pull needed to trigger a refresh feels notably longer than the native iOS/Android gesture, so some users won't discover it.
findings.rootCause: TRIGGER_THRESHOLD_PX is 70 measured AFTER PULL_RESISTANCE=0.5 is applied, i.e. ~140px of actual finger travel; native is ~70-80px.
findings.repro: On mobile, pull down at the top of Home — it takes a long drag to arm the spinner.
findings.suggestedFix: Lower the effective threshold (e.g. threshold 50 or resistance 0.6) and/or add a haptic/visual 'release to refresh' state at the arm point (the is-armed class exists; surface it more).

findings.title: Gesture + keyboard modules are otherwise solid — no data-loss or correctness defects found
findings.symptom: (Coverage note) mobileSwipe/pullToRefresh use disjoint activation cones, modal/map/sidebar opt-outs, single-finger + scrollTop===0 + refresh-in-progress guards; keyboard-avoidance uses the correct visualViewport math. The mobile risk surface is layout/affordance (above), not gesture logic.
findings.rootCause: N/A — documents what was checked and found clean so this dimension isn't mistaken for un-audited.
